sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: CCC+, Moody's: Caa1, Fitch: CCC+

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Pakistan declared 'open war' on Afghanistan on Feb 27, launching Operation Ghazab Lil Haq with airstrikes on Kabul and Kandahar. This represents the most severe escalation in Pakistan-Afghanistan relations since the Taliban takeover, pitting a nuclear-armed state against Taliban-governed Afghanistan. Sovereign credit at CCC+/Caa1 with 1,650 bps risk premium. GDP growth at 2.7% is now under severe downward pressure from defense spending surge and border trade disruption.
key_changes_this_week: Pakistan declared 'open war' on Afghanistan Feb 27; airstrikes hit Kabul and Kandahar targeting TTP/ISIS-K (transformative)
Pakistan rejected all calls for dialogue; Iran, EU, Qatar, and China urged de-escalation
Cross-border trade suspended; refugee flows disrupting border provinces
Defense spending surge will strain already fragile fiscal position ($18.4B FX reserves)
narrative.outlook: Pakistan's fiscal trajectory was already precarious before the war declaration. Defense spending will consume additional budgetary resources, crowding out development expenditure and complicating ongoing IMF program compliance. Border closure with Afghanistan disrupts overland trade routes and creates humanitarian pressures. China's CPEC investments face elevated operational risk. Resolution timeline is unclear given Pakistan's stated refusal of dialogue.
narrative.investor_implications: Pakistan sovereign bonds face further spread widening. CCC+/Caa1 rating at risk of further downgrade if war expenditure depletes FX reserves below critical thresholds. PKR likely under renewed depreciation pressure. Any exposure to Pakistan-linked assets should be reviewed for conflict escalation scenarios.
narrative.data_quality_note: Pakistan macro data is generally of medium quality. FX reserves ($18.4B) are current. War-related fiscal impacts are not yet reflected in published statistics. Conflict casualty and territorial data rely on single-source military reporting.

active_alerts.title: Pakistan declares open war on Afghanistan with airstrikes on Kabul and Kandahar
active_alerts.description: Pakistan declared 'open war' on Afghanistan on Feb 27, 2026, launching airstrikes on Kabul and Kandahar. This represents a dramatic escalation from cross-border tensions to full-scale conflict between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-governed Afghanistan. Pakistan shows strong_decrease trends in 8 of 11 tracked factors. Sovereign credit rating already at CCC+/Caa1 level. Political risk premium elevated to 1,650 bps. War will severely strain Pakistan's already fragile fiscal position and balance of payments.
